raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I was scheduled to travel to XXXX XXXX XXXX in XXXX for a pre-paid hotel stay from XXXX XXXX  2024. However, due to an extreme snowstorm and road closures, it became physically impossible to reach the hotel. The hotel itself admitted in writing that due to severe weather conditions, it was virtually impossible to resell the room. 

Despite this, the hotel kept my full payment of CHF XXXX, even though Swiss contract law explicitly voids contracts where performance is impossible ( Article XXXX CO ) and prohibits unjust enrichment ( Article XXXX CO ) when no service is provided. 

I immediately disputed this charge with American Express on the grounds that : The hotel could not provide the service, making the contract unenforceable under Swiss law. 

The hotel suffered no financial loss since they could not have resold the room. 

Keeping my full payment constitutes unjust enrichment. 

American Express denied my dispute and refused to acknowledge Swiss contract law or consider my legal argument. I requested : A supervisor review, which was denied. 

A written legal explanation for why Amex was upholding an invalid charge, which was ignored. 

Arbitration, as per Amexs cardholder agreement, which they refused. 

Amex repeatedly stated that they only follow the merchants policy, even though the policy is legally unenforceable under Swiss contract law. They then closed my dispute, stating they had no recourse and told me to take the issue up outside of Amex. 

American Express is failing to conduct a fair chargeback review, is enforcing an illegal charge, and is denying arbitration, violating fair dispute resolution practices. I am requesting that the CFPB investigate this improper chargeback handling and compel Amex to refund the CHF XXXX charge.
